items.mistake: Treating "Claude" and "the Claude API" as unrelated products.
items.fix: Think of them as the same model family with different interfaces—chat for humans, Messages API for software.
items.why: Confusion leads teams to re-evaluate vendors when they already have API access, or to expect local-only behavior from a cloud API.
items.example: A PM says "we can't use Claude in our app" while engineers already have Anthropic API keys for another feature.

items.mistake: Assuming the free chat app and the API have identical limits and models.
items.fix: Check plan docs for each surface: chat plans, rate limits, and default models differ from API pricing and model IDs.
items.why: Prototypes built on claude.ai may not translate 1:1 to production API cost or context limits.
items.example: A long document workflow works in a paid chat plan but exceeds token limits when ported to a Haiku API call without chunking.
